title: DraftFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a draft feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setSingleAngle
methods.description: Defines the draft to be defined so that a single angle is used for all drafts. If the isSymmetric is true then the faces are split along the parting plane and drafted independently using the same angle.

methods.name: setTwoAngles
methods.description: Defines both angles to use when the surfaces are split along the draft plane or parting line and the faces on each side of the plane are drafted independently.

properties.name: angleOne
properties.description: Gets the first, or the only angle in the case of a single angle definition.

properties.name: angleTwo
properties.description: Gets the second angle. This can be null in the case where a single angle definition is used.

properties.name: inputFaces
properties.description: Gets and sets the input faces. If IsTangentChain is true, all the faces that are tangentially connected to the input faces (if any) will also be included.

properties.name: isDirectionFlipped
properties.description: Gets and sets if the direction of the draft is flipped. This defaults to false.

properties.name: isSymmetric
properties.description: Gets if the draft is symmetric from the draft plane. This only applies in the case where two angles have been specified and should be ignored otherwise.

properties.name: isTangentChain
properties.description: Gets and sets if any faces that are tangentially connected to any of the input faces will also be included in setting InputEntities. It defaults to true.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: plane
properties.description: Gets and sets the plane that defines the direction in which the draft is applied. This is also referred to as the pull direction. This can be defined using either a planar BRepFace or a ConstructionPlane.

properties.name: targetBaseFeature
properties.description: When creating a feature that is owned by a base feature, set this property to the base feature you want to associate the new feature with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a feature associated with a base feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base feature. The base feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Draft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new draft feature.

angleOne.title: DraftFeatureInput.angleOne Property
angleOne.description: Gets the first, or the only angle in the case of a single angle definition.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = draftFeatureInput_var.angleOne

angleTwo.title: DraftFeatureInput.angleTwo Property
angleTwo.description: Gets the second angle. This can be null in the case where a single angle definition is used.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = draftFeatureInput_var.angleTwo

inputFaces.title: DraftFeatureInput.inputFaces Property
inputFaces.description: Gets and sets the input faces. If IsTangentChain is true, all the faces that are tangentially connected to the input faces (if any) will also be included.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = draftFeatureInput_var.inputFaces

# Set the value of the property.
draftFeatureInput_var.inputFaces = propertyValue

syntax.python:

classType.title: DraftFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setSingleAngle.title: DraftFeatureInput.setSingleAngle Method
setSingleAngle.description: Defines the draft to be defined so that a single angle is used for all drafts. If the isSymmetric is true then the faces are split along the parting plane and drafted independently using the same angle.

parameters.name: isSymmetric
parameters.description: Set to true if the faces are to be split along the plane or parting line and drafted symmetrically. This will have the side effect of setting the isSymmetric property to the same value.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle of the draft. This can be a positive or negative value which will affect the direction of the draft along with the isDirectionFlipped property.

related_samples.name: draftFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the draftFeatures.add method. To use this sample, have a design open that contains at least one body. When you run the sample, you will be prompted to select the face to draft. Because the pull direction is using the base X-Y plane, you need to select a face that is not parallel to the X-Y plane.

related_samples.name: Draft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new draft feature.

setTwoAngles.title: DraftFeatureInput.setTwoAngles Method
setTwoAngles.description: Defines both angles to use when the surfaces are split along the draft plane or parting line and the faces on each side of the plane are drafted independently.

parameters.name: angleOne
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle for the faces on the first side of the draft plane or parting line.

parameters.name: angleTwo
parameters.description: The ValueInput object that defines the angle for the faces on the second side of the draft plane or parting line.
